items.title: Stacks & Queues
items.description: Tree traversals are implemented with an explicit (or implicit) stack for DFS and a queue for BFS.

items.title: Heaps & Priority Queues
items.description: Heaps are complete binary trees with a *heap* invariant (parent ≤ children) rather than the BST invariant — different shape, different use.

items.title: Graphs
items.description: A tree is a connected, acyclic, single-parent graph; graph algorithms (DFS, BFS) work on trees too.

items.title: Recursion
items.description: Most tree algorithms are recursive: 'do something at the root, recurse into children'.

items.title: Big-O Complexity
items.description: Balanced BSTs are the prototypical O(log n) data structure; unbalanced ones illustrate how invariants matter.
